title: Velora Health — post-demo fit & model-delta next steps
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Brooks <sam@velorahealth.com>, Lucy Hendrix <lucy.hendrix@velorahealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jan 2028 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Velora Health — post-demo fit & model-delta next steps

Thanks again for taking the demo today — really helpful session. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: 

- Findings from demo: Velora's triage assistant performs well on surface-level clarification prompts but we saw inconsistent extraction on diagnostic fields (ICD code suggestions and confidence).
- Root cause hypothesis: recent model variant we served to your trial (rw-medium-v3) shows a shift on medical-entity recall vs rw-medium-v2 we originally benchmarked.

Action items I propose (please confirm):
1) Engineering will snapshot the current trial traffic and pin traffic to rw-medium-v2 for the next 48–72 hours so you have stable outputs for your QA. (Requested: engineers Priya + Dev)
2) We'll share the benchmark CSV and the prompt set used in the demo for your QA team to run locally. (attached: velora_benchmarks_v2.csv, prompt_set_v1.txt)
3) We'll run a focused A/B eval (50/50) between v2 and v3 on a 2k-golden-set and report token-level diffs and false-negative ICD extractions. Deliverable: diff report + recommendation. (owner: Marissa)
4) If the diff exceeds the agreed thresholds (see suggested thresholds in the Drive link), we will either pin rw-medium-v2 for your account or configure on-request rollback with a 1-click pin guard.

Please let me know if Sam or Lucy want the pin to happen immediately, or if you prefer we run the A/B first and then decide. I recommend pinning now to give your clinical reviewers a stable baseline while we investigate.

Thanks — Alex

--
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0117


From: Sam Brooks <sam@velorahealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lucy Hendrix <lucy.hendrix@velorahealth.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jan 2028 16:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Velora Health — post-demo fit & model-delta next steps

Thanks Alex — good recap. Lucy and I reviewed a small sample of transcripts and confirm the ICD extraction variance is visible and concerning for our QA reviewers. Two quick asks: 

1) Can you pin to rw-medium-v2 immediately? Our reviewers are mid-run and we don't want further noise in their logs.
2) Please include the raw prompts for the extraction step (we want the exact prompt text and any system messages).

If you pin, we'll continue the A/B after we get the benchmark report. Appreciate the fast turnaround.

—Sam
Sam Brooks
Product Manager, Clinical AI | Velora Health
sam@velorahealth.com


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Sam Brooks <sam@velorahealth.com>, Lucy Hendrix <lucy.hendrix@velorahealth.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jan 2028 17:22:00 -08:00

Sam — understood, will do. Quick notes on ops plan and ETA: 

- I'll schedule the pin now to take effect immediately for Velora's trial tenant. Pinning will route all inference to rw-medium-v2 and create a snapshot artifact of the prior rt variant. We will keep the previous variant available for rollback if needed.
- I'll trigger a 2k A/B run tomorrow (18 Jan) against your golden set with Marissa driving metrics. Expected preliminary diff report by EOD 18 Jan, final by morning 19 Jan.
- Prompt package: attached prompt_set_v1.txt contains the extraction and system prompts used during the demo; also adding a short README in Drive with how we truncate/normalize tokens for the eval.

Dev — please confirm once the pin is in place and paste the route id + snapshot id here.

Priya
Priya Natarajan
Customer Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Brooks <sam@velorahealth.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jan 2028 09:07:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Velora Health — post-demo fit & model-delta next steps

Pinned the account routing for Velora Health to rw-medium-v2 as requested. Details below: 

- Snapshot ID: snap-velora-20280117-01
- Route ID: route-velora-001
- Pin type: account-wide temporary (expires in 72h unless extended)
- A/B harness: A/B job queued (job id: ab-velora-20280118) will run tomorrow at 02:00 UTC against the 2k-golden-set uploaded to the Velora Drive folder.

Notes on what I observed during a quick replay of the failing cases: the v3 variant introduced a tokenization change in our tokenizer config (subword merge threshold) that caused slightly different entity splits for some ICD codes. That explains the recall gap. Will include per-case tokenization diffs in the report.

If you want an immediate rollback instead of a pin, I can do a force-rollback to v2 (imperative); pin preserves the previous variant for auditing.

—Dev
Dev Patel
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference
dev.patel@redwoodinference.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Brooks <sam@velorahealth.com>, Lucy Hendrix <lucy.hendrix@velorahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Jan 2028 09:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Velora Health — post-demo fit & model-delta next steps

Quick update on the A/B: preliminary diff (first 500 cases) shows a 4.1% drop in ICD recall for rw-medium-v3 vs v2, and a 0.9% increase in hallucination tokens measured by our QA heuristics. Full 2k report is uploaded to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1VeloraBenchmarks (file: velora_diff_report_20280118.pdf).

Recommendation: keep the account pinned to rw-medium-v2 until we ship a targeted tokenizer/decoding patch for the v3 line. I propose the following timeline: 
- 19 Jan morning: keep pin in place (Priya/Dev to extend pin expiration to 14 days for stability)
- 20–22 Jan: smallship tokenizer patch and internal canary on rw-medium-v3-fix
- 23 Jan: if internal canary is green, run targeted 1k regression against Velora golden set and then unpin if parity met

Sam/Lucy — can you confirm you want the 14-day pin extension? If yes, Priya/Dev will extend this afternoon and we'll treat this as the official mitigation.

Thanks — Marissa
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

title: Dedicated capacity sizing — memory profile and quantization implications for HelixThera
preview:
From: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity ask — context & quant questions

Evelyn / Marcus —

Thanks again for the call this morning. We’re finalizing the procurement bulletin and need a clear recommendation on GPU sizing tied to two production usage patterns:

1) Interactive clinician assistant (median prompts ~600 tokens, occasional docs up to 20k tokens).
2) Batch inference for nightly retraining/embedding generation (large batches, tolerant to latency).

Two model variants on the table: L3-70B (32k window) and L3-13B (32k). Team is especially concerned about the KV cache size with longer contexts and whether quantization (fp16 -> int8) materially reduces your per-instance GPU needs.

Can you provide a recommended Dedicated pool footprint (GPU type + count), a short rationale, and any quick mitigation we should include in the SOW (e.g., prefix caching, offload targets)?

Looking to finalize numbers by Monday. Attachment: initial usage traces and latency SLOs.

—Samir
HelixThera, Head of AI Ops
[Attachment: helix_usage_traces_2027-11-19.csv (csv, 120 KB)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 11:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — context & quant questions

Samir —

Quick acknowledgement: we can get you a concise footprint and tradeoff table. Karthik and Priya will run a focused memory simulation based on your traces; I’ll pull together reserved pool sizing and a recommended canary/burst plan for the SOW.

Preliminary thoughts: if most interactive traffic stays <4k tokens, 70B with aggressive int8 can fit more densely; but the 20k spikes are the cost-driver because KV cache grows roughly linearly with sequence length. We’ll send numbers by COB today.

Marcus
Sr. Solutions Eng, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: none]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 16:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — memory math and quantization impact (draft)

Samir / team —

I ran a short simulation against the traces you attached. Key assumptions up front:
- Models: L3-70B and L3-13B with 32k context window.
- We model weights memory (FP16) and KV cache separately. KV cache estimates assume 2 streams (encoder+decoder style) per token where relevant; we also account for attention key+value sizes.
- GPU targets: NVIDIA H100 80GB and A100 80GB (we expect you to prefer H100 where latency and throughput matter).

High-level numbers (per active model instance, peak during a long-context request):
- L3-70B (FP16 weights): weights ~34 GB; KV cache @ 32k context peak ~30–34 GB (depends on tokenization and past context reuse). Total ~64–68 GB.
- L3-70B (8-bit quantized weights int8): weights compress to ~18–20 GB; KV cache unchanged (~30–34 GB). Total ~48–54 GB.
- L3-13B (FP16): weights ~6.5 GB; KV cache @ 32k ~30–34 GB; total ~36–41 GB.
- L3-13B (int8): weights ~3.5 GB; KV cache unchanged; total ~33–37 GB.

Implications:
- For long-context spikes (near 32k), even aggressive int8 quant only reduces the weight footprint — KV cache dominates memory usage. That means the choice of GPU with >64 GB device memory gives the most headroom for 70B serving without offload.
- On H100 80GB you can host one L3-70B int8 instance per GPU with ~20% headroom under peak; FP16 70B risks memory eviction unless we offload or shard.
- L3-13B is much cheaper; you can get 1–2 int8 L3-13B instances per 80GB GPU while still accommodating KV cache for long-context requests.

Operational sizing recommendation (baseline production footprint):
- If primary interactive workload uses L3-70B for high-quality responses: reserve an H100 80GB pool sized for concurrency RPS = 40 concurrent interactive sessions -> map to 12–14 H100s assuming per-instance memory headroom and accounting for batching and prefetching. This includes ~20% buffer for peaks and canary traffic.
- If you standardize on L3-13B for cost-sensitive paths (embeddings / shorter responses) add a 6–8 GPU burst pool (A100/H100) for scaling batch workloads.

Mitigations to reduce dedicated footprint:
- Prefix/KV caching for repeated system prompts and common document prefixes (saves KV memory for many requests).
- Lightweight offload (CPU or NVMe) for cold KV pages; useful if long-context spikes are rare.
- Hybrid routing: route long 32k requests to a small shared high-memory pool (fewer reserved GPUs, billed as burst) while keeping the majority on smaller reserved capacity.

I’ll attach the CSV with token-level memory curves. If you want, we can run a short canary with live traffic samples to validate these numbers next week.

—Karthik
Principal Applied ML Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: kv_cache_sim.csv (csv, 45 KB)]

From: Laura Benoit <laura.benoit@helixthera.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 10:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity sizing — memory profile and quantization implications for HelixThera

Karthik / team —

Thanks, this is very helpful context. A couple of questions before we lock numbers:

1) You mentioned offload to NVMe: what is the expected latency penalty for a 20k token request if 30% of KV is cold and must be loaded from NVMe? We need that for our SLA table.
2) For the recommended 12–14 H100s: is that assuming single tenancy per GPU or can we colocate smaller models/replicas to improve utilization?

We’d like the SOW to include a plan to start with a 6 GPU reserved pool (cost anchor) + a burst lane for long-context requests and then expand to the 12–14 H100s if monthly P95 latency or concurrency thresholds are hit. Can Redwood provide the burst autoscale playbook?

Thanks —Laura
VP, Product Engineering, HelixThera
[Attachment: none]

On Fri, Nov 20, 2027 at 4:28 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:

> 
> I ran a short simulation against the traces you attached...

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>, Laura Benoit <laura.benoit@helixthera.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 16:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity sizing — memory profile and quantization implications for HelixThera

Laura / Samir —

Appreciate the quick follow-up. To answer your questions and propose next steps: 

1) NVMe offload latency: for the pattern you described (20k tokens, ~30% cold KV pages) expect an additional 120–250 ms p95 add-on if we use a warm NVMe tier (low-latency NVMe on the instance). If offload goes to network-attached NVMe the penalty can be 300–700 ms depending on NIC/network topology. We’ll quantify precise values in the canary.

2) Colocation: smaller models (L3-13B int8) can be colocated on the same 80GB device alongside a single L3-70B int8 instance only if sufficient headroom remains — this reduces cost but complicates isolation/SLA. Our conservative SOW recommendation is single-tenancy for primary interactive 70B traffic, with co-located replicas for cost-sensitive batch lanes. We’ll provide a placement matrix in the SOW.

Proposed SOW milestones:
- Phase 0 (pilot, 2 weeks): 6 H100s reserved, run canary on 2 H100s with mirrored traffic to measure NVMe offload latency and real concurrency memory behavior.
- Phase 1 (production baseline, month 1): expand to 8 H100s + 6 A100 burst pool for batch and embedding workloads.
- Phase 2 (scale): expand to 12–14 H100s if P95 latency > target or concurrency > 35 sustained per minute.

Action items assigned: Marcus (deploy canary config + autoscale playbook), Karthik (finalize per-token memory curves + NVMe latency microbench), Priya (draft SOW language for reserved vs burst capacity and cost model).

I’ll attach a short slide summary and send a SOW draft by Wed EOD. If HelixThera confirms the pilot window we’ll schedule the canary run.

Thanks —Evelyn
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference

Evelyn Hart
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference

From: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity ask — context & quant questions

Evelyn / Marcus —

Thanks again for the call this morning. We’re finalizing the procurement bulletin and need a clear recommendation on GPU sizing tied to two production usage patterns:

1) Interactive clinician assistant (median prompts ~600 tokens, occasional docs up to 20k tokens).
2) Batch inference for nightly retraining/embedding generation (large batches, tolerant to latency).

Two model variants on the table: L3-70B (32k window) and L3-13B (32k). Team is especially concerned about the KV cache size with longer contexts and whether quantization (fp16 -> int8) materially reduces your per-instance GPU needs.

Can you provide a recommended Dedicated pool footprint (GPU type + count), a short rationale, and any quick mitigation we should include in the SOW (e.g., prefix caching, offload targets)?

Looking to finalize numbers by Monday. Attachment: initial usage traces and latency SLOs.

—Samir
HelixThera, Head of AI Ops
[Attachment: helix_usage_traces_2027-11-19.csv (csv, 120 KB)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 11:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — context & quant questions

Samir —

Quick acknowledgement: we can get you a concise footprint and tradeoff table. Karthik and Priya will run a focused memory simulation based on your traces; I’ll pull together reserved pool sizing and a recommended canary/burst plan for the SOW.

Preliminary thoughts: if most interactive traffic stays <4k tokens, 70B with aggressive int8 can fit more densely; but the 20k spikes are the cost-driver because KV cache grows roughly linearly with sequence length. We’ll send numbers by COB today.

Marcus
Sr. Solutions Eng, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: none]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@helixthera.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2027 16:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity ask — memory math and quantization impact (draft)

Samir / team —

I ran a short simulation against the traces you attached. Key assumptions up front:
- Models: L3-70B and L3-13B with 32k context window.
- We model weights memory (FP16) and KV cache separately. KV cache estimates assume 2 streams (encoder+decoder style) per token where relevant; we also account for attention key+value sizes.
- GPU targets: NVIDIA H100 80GB and A100 80GB (we expect you to prefer H100 where latency and throughput matter).

High-level numbers (per active model instance, peak during a long-context request):
- L3-70B (FP16 weights): weights ~34 GB; KV cache @ 32k context peak ~30–34 GB (depends on tokenization and past context reuse). Total ~64–68 GB.
- L3-70B (8-bit quantized weights int8): weights compress to ~18–20 GB; KV cache unchanged (~30–34 GB). Total ~48–54 GB.
- L3-13B (FP16): weights ~6.5 GB; KV cache @ 32k ~30–34 GB; total ~36–41 GB.
- L3-13B (int8): weights ~3.5 GB; KV cache unchanged; total ~33–37 GB.

Implications:
- For long-context spikes (near 32k), even aggressive int8 quant only reduces the weight footprint — KV cache dominates memory usage. That means the choice of GPU with >64 GB device memory gives the most headroom for 70B serving without offload.
- On H100 80GB you can host one L3-70B int8 instance per GPU with ~20% headroom under peak; FP16 70B risks memory eviction unless we offload or shard.
- L3-13B is much cheaper; you can get 1–2 int8 L3-13B instances per 80GB GPU while still accommodating KV cache for long-context requests.

Operational sizing recommendation (baseline production footprint):
- If primary interactive workload uses L3-70B for high-quality responses: reserve an H100 80GB pool sized for concurrency RPS = 40 concurrent interactive sessions -> map to 12–14 H100s assuming per-instance memory headroom and accounting for batching and prefetching. This includes ~20% buffer for peaks and canary traffic.
- If you standardize on L3-13B for cost-sensitive paths (embeddings / shorter responses) add a 6–8 GPU burst pool (A100/H100) for scaling batch workloads.

Mitigations to reduce dedicated footprint:
- Prefix/KV caching for repeated system prompts and common document prefixes (saves KV memory for many requests).
- Lightweight offload (CPU or NVMe) for cold KV pages; useful if long-context spikes are rare.
- Hybrid routing: route long 32k requests to a small shared high-memory pool (fewer reserved GPUs, billed as burst) while keeping the majority on smaller reserved capacity.

I’ll attach the CSV with token-level memory curves. If you want, we can run a short canary with live traffic samples to validate these numbers next week.

—Karthik
Principal Applied ML Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: kv_cache_sim.csv (csv, 45 KB)]

title: EU VAT / billing entity split — PO fields, remit routing, and Net45 request
preview:
From: Linda Park <linda.park@medivance.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@medivance.com>, Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@medivance.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2027 13:12:00 +0000
Subject: PO requirements & billing questions for Redwood (MediVance)

Irene —

Thanks for the demo last week. Before we lock in the SOW, procurement needs to confirm a few billing/pricing constraints so our AP team can process Redwood invoices without delays. Can you confirm whether Redwood can accommodate the following for the MediVance account?

Required items from our side:
- PO number must be present in the invoice header and in the invoice filename; our ERP enforces exact match.
- Each invoice must include the billing entity name and VAT/tax ID in a discrete field (we operate across two EU entities).
- Remit-to address varies by country; we need the invoice to support a per-line remit-to mapping or at minimum a clear remit-to field.
- Net45 payment terms (this is the org standard for vendor onboarding).
- We ask for weekly invoices (every Friday) that roll up to a monthly consolidated statement for reconciliation.
- For certain services we require reverse-charge VAT notation.

We can consider a small prepayment or milestone-based advance if it helps, but AP has asked procurement to get the standard behavior documented. Attaching a sample PO for reference (MediVance_PO_sample.pdf).

Can you confirm what flexibility exists on packaging (1) consolidated monthly billing vs per-entity invoices, and (2) whether Redwood can accept Net45 as a permanent term? Also please let us know if providing a VAT-exempt/reverse-charge line is possible.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 30m call if that’s easier.

Best,
Linda Park
Director, Procurement
MediVance Health



From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Linda Park <linda.park@medivance.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2027 17:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: PO requirements & billing questions for Redwood (MediVance)

Hi Linda —

Appreciate the clear list, very helpful. Short answers up front: we can support the PO number and VAT/tax-id fields and can include remit-to on the invoice. Net45 is possible but needs a quick finance approval for the account (we typically default to Net30). Weekly invoices that roll into a consolidated monthly statement are doable — we can emit weekly invoice items and provide a month-end statement.

A few follow-ups so we take the right approach:
- Is the remit-to address different per invoice line (rare) or per billing entity? If per-entity, we’ll map remit-to to the billing entity on each invoice.
- For reverse-charge, can you confirm which of the two EU entities will require reverse-charge on an ongoing basis? We’ll add a configurable VAT note but need to map it by entity.
- On Net45: finance would like to see whether you can accept any trade (e.g., a 3–5% prepayment, or an annual commit) in exchange for the extended terms. If Net45 must be unconditional, we’ll escalate for an exception.

I’m attaching a PO-template.xlsx and our redlined MasterServiceAgreement_v4.pdf for your legal/ops teams to review; these show how we render VAT and remit fields on Redwood invoices.

Proposed next steps:
1) We’ll confirm with Finance (cc’d Karthik and Priya) on Net45 exception workflow.
2) If you can share the exact VAT IDs and remit mapping, we’ll produce a sample invoice PDF for your AP team to validate.
3) Optional: 30m call to finalize logistics — are you available Tues 10–11am CET?

I’ll follow up as soon as I hear from finance.

Thanks,

Irene Choi
Senior Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0134



From: Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@medivance.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Linda Park <linda.park@medivance.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@medivance.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Nov 2027 08:26:00 +0000
Subject: Re: PO requirements & billing questions for Redwood (MediVance)

Irene —

Thanks for the quick reply. A couple clarifications from AP / tax:
- Remit-to is per billing entity (not per-line). We run two legal entities (MediVance GmbH — DE VAT ID: DE123456789; MediVance Ireland — IE VAT ID: IE987654321). Invoices must show the correct VAT ID on the invoice header for the entity being billed.
- Reverse-charge will apply for services billed to the Irish entity only.
- Net45 is a firm requirement for this supplier cohort; we cannot move to Net30 on our end.
- Weekly invoices are our preference because it speeds up booking; the consolidated monthly statement is used for reconciliation only.

We cannot do large prepayments at signing, but we could do a small 1–2% upfront on the first invoice if that materially helps your approval. If you must escalate for Net45, please do — we need confirmation before legal and procurement sign the PO.

Attached: sample PO and a short AP checklist (MediVance_PO_sample.pdf). Also, could you send a sample invoice PDF with the DE and IE VAT rows populated so our AP team can verify ERP parsing?

Regards,
Marco Rossi
Sourcing Manager — EU
MediVance Health

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>

Marco / Linda —

Thanks for confirming the remit/VAT split and the firm Net45 requirement — that’s very clear and helpful. Quick summary and next actions from Redwood: 

Summary of what we’ll do:
- Invoice formatting: we’ll include a discrete Billing Entity field and VAT/Tax ID in the invoice header. We’ll render a reverse-charge VAT note for the Irish entity when applicable.
- Remit-to: mapped by billing entity (DE vs IE) on each invoice.
- PO: we will populate the PO number in the invoice header and filename to match your ERP enforcement.
- Invoice cadence: we will emit weekly invoices (every Friday) with a consolidated month-end statement for reconciliation.
- Net45: I’ve opened an exception request with Finance for the Net45 default for this account. Karthik / Priya — can you weigh in on the approval timeline? If helpful, MediVance has offered a 1–2% upfront on the first invoice; we can factor that into the credit assessment.

Next steps / ask list:
1) Marco — please share the exact VAT IDs and a quick remit mapping CSV (entity -> remit address) so we can complete the sample.
2) Karthik / Priya — please review Net45 escalation; if approved we’ll capture Net45 in the billing config and SOW. If Finance needs a minimal trade (small prepay or annual commit), I’ll loop back to MediVance with options.
3) I will send two sample invoice PDFs (one billed to DE entity, one to IE entity) by EOD Wed for your AP team to test ERP parsing.

Propose a short alignment call to confirm details: Tue 16 Nov, 10:00–10:30 CET — does that work? If yes, I’ll send a calendar invite and include finance.

Thanks — we’ll move quickly on the sample invoices.

Best,

Irene Choi
Senior Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference

title: Pilot addendum: Timebox booster vs token voucher options
preview:
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@finora.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick ask: 2-week pilot extension + engineering help?

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the run so far — the team has been iterating on prompts and the model behavior is improving. We hit a couple of integration snags this week (prefix caching mismatch + a telemetry hook issue) and would like to request a short extension to the timeboxed pilot so we can validate the fixes in our staging environment.

Ask: would Redwood consider a 14-day extension to our test window, plus some engineering support (ideally 20–40 hours) to help triage and confirm the KV/prefix behavior? Our success criteria are:

title: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Luca Rossi <luca@fintegrate.io>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate

Hi Luca,

Quick follow-up from our onboarding call — Fintegrate is listed as a downstream processor in the DPA we sent, and Legal has asked for a short reconciliation of your current third-party roster and the specific transfer routes (e.g., EU->US via subprocessor X, EU->EEA via partner Y) that you rely on for data necessary to serve our integration.

Could you:
- Share the most recent vendor roster (CSV) with columns for vendor name, function, country of processing, and whether they have access to customer data or only metadata.
- Confirm any persistent cross-border transfers from the EU for logs/backups/analytics and the legal basis (SCCs/EEA contracts/other).
- If there are planned vendor changes in the next 90 days, flag them and indicate whether they touch customer content.

If it helps, I attached the template we use to intake vendor rosters. Aim for the roster and a short note by COB PT Monday so Legal can clear the path for our SOC review.

Thanks —

Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales (Platform)
Redwood Inference
[attachment: vendor_roster_template.csv (CSV, 12KB)]

From: Luca Rossi <luca@fintegrate.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: compliance@fintegrate.io
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2027 12:05:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate

Avery —

Apologies for the delay; thanks for the clear checklist. Attached are: vendor_roster_v2.csv (our current inventory), a high-level dataflow diagram, and our SCC submission packet. Highlights below:

- Vendor roster (see vendor_roster_v2.csv): we mark vendors with access to customer content in the "access_level" column.
- EU transfers: primary cross-border transfer is to our analytics provider (NorthMetrics) for aggregated usage metrics — they only receive pseudonymized metrics. Backups are stored in Azure West US; backups contain encrypted blobs (customer keys managed by KMS).
- Legal basis: SCCs in place with NorthMetrics and Azure; DocuSign link to executed SCCs included.
- Planned changes: migrating part of telemetry processing to EdgeSight (Q1 2028). EdgeSight will only handle aggregated telemetry and will not receive content-level data.

Let me know if you need raw samples or a short attestation from our DPO.

Cheers,

Luca Rossi
Head of Integrations
Fintegrate
[attachment: vendor_roster_v2.csv (CSV, 48KB)]
[attachment: dataflow-diagram.png (PNG, 240KB)]
[attachment: Fintegrate_SCC_submission.pdf (PDF, 320KB)]

- Azure backups: confirm whether keys are customer-managed HSM (we need an attestation if not).
- EdgeSight migration: request a short statement that EdgeSight will only process aggregated telemetry and never ingest customer content.

Priya — can you take point on the two attestation asks and the SCC check? I added the Drive link to the attachments in the related_links list.

Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Luca Rossi <luca@fintegrate.io>, compliance@fintegrate.io
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2027 14:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate

Hi Luca — thanks for the materials, and for calling out EdgeSight. A couple of narrow asks to close the ticket from our side: 

1) NorthMetrics: can you provide a one-paragraph technical attestation stating the pseudonymization/hashing approach plus retention window for the aggregated metrics? A short code/data flow snippet is helpful but not required.

2) Azure backups: please confirm whether backup encryption keys are managed by Fintegrate (customer-managed) or by Azure (platform-managed). If platform-managed, we need a short justification and confirmation of the key rotation cadence.

3) EdgeSight migration: please append a brief statement from your DPO or engineering lead that EdgeSight will not process or store customer content and describe the telemetry schema that will be sent (fields/PII/no-PII).

If you can drop these three items into a short PDF or email by EOD PT Monday, Legal will do a quick review and we can move the SOC-connection checklist forward.

Thanks again — we appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,

Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

title: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: C. Thomas <cthomas@archimedes.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-09-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification

Hi Chris —

Quick note ahead of your Q4 renewal: thank you again for the heads-up about the increased inference footprint. We ran a short bench for your core image-captioning pipeline against our A100 baseline and two upgrade paths (H100 and L40S).

High level asks so we can put a clean amendment on your desk:
- Confirm target reserved capacity (additional GPUs) and desired 95th/99th throughput targets.
- Preferred hardware mix (all H100, H100 + L40S economy mix, or keep A100 for part of the pool).
- Whether you want a formal throughput guarantee line-item tied to credits/uptime.

I'll loop in Ben (SE) and Marissa (CS) to produce: short PPD model, recommended H100/L40S split, and a proposed SLA amendment that includes a classical throughput guardrail (tokens/sec per pool) + fallback policy.

Proposed immediate next step: 30-minute review call this week to walk the numbers and an amendment draft (I can do Thu morning).

Thanks —
Evelyn Hart
Account Executive – Redwood Inference
E: evelyn.hart@redwood.ai | M: +1 415-555-0197

Attachments: proposed-amendment-AR2481.pdf (preview)



From: C. Thomas <cthomas@archimedes.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Geller <alex.geller@archimedes.ai>
Date: 2027-09-21T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification

Evelyn —

Thanks. We’re aiming to double our reserved token throughput for the Q4 campaign window; rough target is +40 GPUs on top of our current pool (currently all A100s).

Key constraints from our side:
- Cost per 1M tokens is still an important metric for the business case.
- We need a firm 95th percentile tokens/sec guarantee for the daytime batch window (Mon-Fri 08:00–20:00 PT) and a soft best-effort overnight window.
- Migration must not impact inference quality (we use fp16 + limited q4_0 quantization).

Open to H100 if the PPD works out — also curious about L40S for less latency-critical workloads. Call Thu works for us; 10:00 PT?

— Chris
Chris Thomas, Head of Infrastructure
Archimedes AI
cthomas@archimedes.ai

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-09-29T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification

Team / Evelyn / Chris (cc’d below) —

I ran the short perf and cost model against Archimedes’ top-10 models (image-captioning + reranker mix you described). Summary in plain bullets; full numbers attached (ppd_modeling_h100_v_a100.csv).

Quick findings:
- H100 (scheduling/kv-cache and compiled kernels) gives ~1.7x median throughput vs A100 for their workload and ~1.9x at the 95th percentile under continuous-batching.
- L40S shows ~1.45x median throughput vs A100 but with ~15% lower hourly cost than H100 in our reserved pricing assumptions.
- When we account for batching gains and cross-request KV reuse (their traffic has high prefix locality), effective tokens-per-second-per-dollar improves:
  - A100 baseline: 1.0 (normalized)
  - H100 path: 1.9x PPD improvement (after kernel tuning + dynamic batching)
  - Hybrid H100(60%)/L40S(40%) mix: ~1.6x PPD improvement vs A100 but ~12% cheaper total $/month than all-H100 for equivalent daytime throughput.

Risk notes / operational considerations:
- Quantization: their current q4_0 profile is supported, but we recommend a short eval to validate sample-level parity (we have a regression checklist).
- Cold-start KV warming on a fresh pool can affect early-day 95th pct; propose a 2-week rolling warm-up with staged traffic shift.
- Throughput guardrail mechanics: we propose a guaranteed tokens/sec baseline measured as a 95th percentile per-hour window, with a credit schedule if missed (credits tied to per-GPU hourly rate, capped monthly). Fallback: auto-failover to a cross-region spare pool (best-effort) or degrade to lower-cost quantized model variant with throttled tokens/sec.

See attached CSV for per-model perf curves and the proposed amendment draft (I attached the short amendment skeleton per Evelyn's marketing doc).

Attaching: ppd_modeling_h100_v_a100.csv, throughput-guardrails-summary.docx

— Ben Carter
Sales Engineer – Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, C. Thomas <cthomas@archimedes.ai>
Date: 2027-10-03T15:44:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification

Chris / Alex —

Thanks for the time last week. Following internal review and Ben’s modeling, here’s the recommended path and next actions:

Recommendation (executive summary):
- Go with a hybrid reserved pool: 24 H100 GPUs + 16 L40S GPUs (adds 40 GPUs total). Rationale: H100 handles latency-sensitive heavy models; L40S provides cost-effective scale for batch/throughput-heavy jobs.
- We will commit to a daytime 95th-percentile tokens/sec guarantee for the pool (Mon–Fri 08:00–20:00 PT). Measurement windows are hourly; missed guarantees trigger credits (calculation in amendment).
- Migration plan: staged roll (20% traffic -> 50% -> 100%) over two weeks with a 72-hour observation between stages. We'll warm KV caches with a controlled replay to prevent early-day regressions.

Key commercial terms (draft):
- Amendment: add +40 GPU reserved capacity for 12 months (term aligned to existing contract renewal date).
- Hardware swap pricing: all-H100 list rate with hybrid discount: hybrid mix priced at 92% of all-H100 list for equivalent daytime throughput.
- Penalty/credit: credits equal to X% of the hourly reserved rate for the proportion of hours missed, capped at 10% of monthly reserved fees. (Finance to finalize).

Attachments: proposed-amendment-AR2481.pdf (clean), ppd_modeling_h100_v_a100.csv, throughput-guardrails-summary.docx

Next steps:
1) Finance (Priya) to confirm discount floor and credit cap wording by EOD Tue 2027-10-05.
2) Legal to review amendment text and send for signatures (we'll push DocuSign link).
3) Technical onboarding call with Ben + Archimedes infra to schedule the canary and KV-warm replay (target week of 10/11).

If that all sounds good I’ll circulate the DocuSign draft and lock a 10/11 onboarding slot.

Best —
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Lead – Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Primary ask for this thread: prepare a concise recommendation (H100 vs H100+L40S mix), a performance-per-dollar (PPD) delta table vs A100, and a proposed amendment clause for throughput guardrails (what we can commit to, fallback policy, and penalty/credits approach).
>
> Call with them Thu 2027-09-23 10:00 PT. Ben — can you bring the bench numbers and the csv PPD model? Marissa — please draft the amendment skeleton with the uptime/throughput clause we usually use, and include a rolling canary/traffic-shift playbook. Priya — flag any finance constraints for discount tiers on reserved swaps.
>
> Thanks —
> Evelyn



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, C. Thomas <cthomas@archimedes.ai>
Date: 2027-10-03T15:44:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated pool renewal: throughput guardrails + hardware regrade PPD justification

Chris / Alex —

Thanks for the time last week. Following internal review and Ben’s modeling, here’s the recommended path and next actions:

Recommendation (executive summary):
- Go with a hybrid reserved pool: 24 H100 GPUs + 16 L40S GPUs (adds 40 GPUs total). Rationale: H100 handles latency-sensitive heavy models; L40S provides cost-effective scale for batch/throughput-heavy jobs.
- We will commit to a daytime 95th-percentile tokens/sec guarantee for the pool (Mon–Fri 08:00–20:00 PT). Measurement windows are hourly; missed guarantees trigger credits (calculation in amendment).
- Migration plan: staged roll (20% traffic -> 50% -> 100%) over two weeks with a 72-hour observation between stages. We'll warm KV caches with a controlled replay to prevent early-day regressions.

Key commercial terms (draft):
- Amendment: add +40 GPU reserved capacity for 12 months (term aligned to existing contract renewal date).
- Hardware swap pricing: all-H100 list rate with hybrid discount: hybrid mix priced at 92% of all-H100 list for equivalent daytime throughput.
- Penalty/credit: credits equal to X% of the hourly reserved rate for the proportion of hours missed, capped at 10% of monthly reserved fees. (Finance to finalize).

Attachments: proposed-amendment-AR2481.pdf (clean), ppd_modeling_h100_v_a100.csv, throughput-guardrails-summary.docx

Next steps:
1) Finance (Priya) to confirm discount floor and credit cap wording by EOD Tue 2027-10-05.
2) Legal to review amendment text and send for signatures (we'll push DocuSign link).
3) Technical onboarding call with Ben + Archimedes infra to schedule the canary and KV-warm replay (target week of 10/11).

If that all sounds good I’ll circulate the DocuSign draft and lock a 10/11 onboarding slot.

Best —
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Lead – Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

On Sep 29, 2027, at 02:22 PM, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team / Evelyn / Chris (cc’d below) —
>
> I ran the short perf and cost model against Archimedes’ top-10 models (image-captioning + reranker mix you described). Summary in plain bullets; full numbers attached (ppd_modeling_h100_v_a100.csv).
>
> Quick findings:
> - H100 (scheduling/kv-cache and compiled kernels) gives ~1.7x median throughput vs A100 for their workload and ~1.9x at the 95th percentile under continuous-batching.
> - L40S shows ~1.45x median throughput vs A100 but with ~15% lower hourly cost than H100 in our reserved pricing assumptions.
>
> — Ben

Quick technical plan from infra:
- Export format: gzipped JSONL for event logs (schema v2), CSV for ACL timeline, and a lightweight index (parquet) for auditor queries.
- Retention/export window: we can produce a contiguous 90-day raw dump + a 12-month event index.
- Estimated size: ~1.8 TB for 90 days (based on their current ingestion; we’ll provide a sampled 48 hr export to validate).
- Transfer options:
  1) Direct S3 put to acme-security-bucket (recommended). We will create a short-lived write role and pre-signed manifests. SHA256 included per object.
  2) Encrypted SFTP drop (for compliance teams that block external buckets).
- Impact: bulk export will consume transient egress and CPU; to keep prod steady we propose a 3-night staggered window (3 x 8-hour windows) outside 02:00–06:00 PDT.
- ACL history: we can export a changelog (CSV) going back 18 months, with user ids and timestamps.

Next steps I can own: produce a 48-hour sample export by Wed 15 Sep, provide exact object counts + signed role for S3. ETA: sample by 2027-09-15 17:00 PDT.

Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 14:32:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: SOC 2 renewal — evidence staging & capacity uplift coordination

Quick technical plan from infra:
- Export format: gzipped JSONL for event logs (schema v2), CSV for ACL timeline, and a lightweight index (parquet) for auditor queries.
- Retention/export window: we can produce a contiguous 90-day raw dump + a 12-month event index.
- Estimated size: ~1.8 TB for 90 days (based on their current ingestion; we’ll provide a sampled 48 hr export to validate).
- Transfer options:
  1) Direct S3 put to acme-security-bucket (recommended). We will create a short-lived write role and pre-signed manifests. SHA256 included per object.
  2) Encrypted SFTP drop (for compliance teams that block external buckets).
- Impact: bulk export will consume transient egress and CPU; to keep prod steady we propose a 3-night staggered window (3 x 8-hour windows) outside 02:00–06:00 PDT.
- ACL history: we can export a changelog (CSV) going back 18 months, with user ids and timestamps.

Next steps I can own: produce a 48-hour sample export by Wed 15 Sep, provide exact object counts + signed role for S3. ETA: sample by 2027-09-15 17:00 PDT.

Karthik Iyer
Senior Infrastructure Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
Attachment: logs_export_sample.tar.gz (sample 48-hour export, gzipped JSONL sample + manifest)

   - Redwood will deliver: soc2_evidence_index.xlsx (artifact list, redaction notes), redwood_s3_manifest.csv (object-level manifest with SHA256), and logs_export_sample.tar.gz (48-hr sample).
   - Delivery method: direct S3 upload to acme-security-bucket using a short-lived write role; Karthik to create the role and share IAM details.
   - Timeline: sample by 2027-09-15; full 90-day export staged across 2027-09-22 to 2027-09-24 (3 nights).

2) Capacity & commercials
   - Acme confirmed +40% seat uplift (reserved capacity) with a 12-month term extension. Marcus will follow up today with the amendment draft and DocuSign link for signature.

3) ACL history & control artifacts
   - Redwood to include ACL changelog CSV (18 months) and change-history for access control lists as requested by auditors. Priya will confirm redaction rules where PII is present.

   - Audit team will receive parquet index files for quick queries. Redwood will preserve checksums and provide MD5/SHA256 manifests for verification.

- Marcus to send the commercial amendment + DocuSign today.
- Priya to confirm PII redaction checklist by 2027-09-16.

Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Ground transit: Workshop day — staging, shuttles, and parking passes

Team + Olivia —

Quick recap and asks for the Aug 31 enterprise workshop at The Plex (Downtown): we expect ~160 attendees, 8 panelists, and 12 on-site staff/AV crew. Key objectives:
- Secure a staging area for 2 shuttle buses and 6 car-service vehicles for guest drop-off (7:30–8:45am peak).
- Reserve 50 parking passes for attendees (venue lot + overflow lot) and 6 passes for rental/AV vehicles all day.
- Confirm ADA-accessible vehicles and one stretcher-capable vehicle for speaker A.
- Provide a meet-and-greet point (hotel-side entrance) and a driver check-in table with radio/walkie contact.
- Provide a quoted invoice and an itemized rate-card we can attach to the vendor PO.

Please reply with: staging coordinates on the site map, loading/unloading procedures (for large equipment), driver contact numbers, and a draft invoice by EOD so we can route to procurement. Priya, Marcus — can you review the map and confirm the preferred overflow lot?

Thanks,
Diego
Diego Fernandez
Events Program Manager | Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0172

[attachment: workshop_agenda.pdf (PDF, 240KB)]

From: Olivia Green <olivia@quadrantevents.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: venue@theplexevents.com
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 09:24:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Ground transit: Workshop day — staging, shuttles, and parking passes

Hi Diego / team —

Thanks for the details. Attached is a proposed site map with staging and two shuttle loops called out, plus a preliminary rate card and parking pass workbook. Highlights:
- Staging: Lot C (north loading dock) can hold 2 buses and up to 8 sedans; recommended shuttle pickup is the curb outside Entrance B (hotel drop-off).
- Shuttle cadence: every 12 minutes between 7:30–9:00am, then on-demand 11:30–1:30pm return windows. We can add an evening shuttle if needed.
- ADA: We will reserve one ramp-equipped minibus and one stretcher-capable vehicle; we've flagged driver Jorge R. as point for accessibility needs.
- Loading: heavy AV trucks should use Dock 4 (backstage) with a 15-minute unload window; dock staff will run a radio monitor at channel 6.
- Pricing: shuttle loop (round trip) $180 per loop; car service (per vehicle) $65/hr (includes 15% staffing surcharge for early staging); parking passes $12 each (venue lot).

I attached site_map.pdf, vendor_rate_card.pdf, and parking_passes.xlsx. Ben Turner from CityCar is looping in to provide the driver roster and confirm contract language.

Olivia Green
Senior Account Manager, Quadrant Events
O: 617-555-3434 | M: 617-555-9102

[attachment: vendor_rate_card.pdf (PDF, 72KB)]

title: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window
preview:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, John Doe <john.doe@acmehealth.com>
Date: 2027-06-18T08:23:00-07:00
Subject: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window

Team —

Acme Health (John DD'ed) has a SOC2 readiness audit on 2027-07-01. They just submitted an evidence request asking for a 30-day export of audit logs and a temporary retention carveout for a specific tenant while they complete their audit. Two bits of complexity:

1) We have a scheduled KMS rotation for the dedicated pool that overlaps their audit window (rotation planned 2027-06-29).
2) Our SSO (SAML) migration for the Dedicated plane is currently scheduled 2027-06-30 -> 07-02 (cutover weekend).

Asking to triage and decide ASAP: can we provide an evidence staging path that preserves immutable audit logs for Acme during the rotation and SSO cutover? If not fully possible, what exception language can we give Acme and Legal that limits scope and duration (they're only asking for a 30-day export for tenant acme-prod)?

Attached: \"kms-rotation-plan-2027.pdf\" and Acme's request doc.

High-level questions:
- Priya: what risk does rotation during the audit present to evidence integrity?
- Marcus: can we snapshot the KV/cache and export a compressed audit bundle that ties to a key escrow?
- Marissa: product ask — what UI/console flow + observability do we need to accept/track an exception?

I want quick proposals by EOD so we can respond to Procurement and Legal.

Thanks,
Jonas
Jonas Weber
Head of Security Partnerships
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, John Doe <john.doe@acmehealth.com>
Date: 2027-06-18T11:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window

Thanks Jonas — quick triage notes and a proposed path forward:

Summary of constraints:
- Acme requires an official, time-boxed artifact for SOC2. They need logs for 30 days and a signed attestation that logs were not modified during export.
- Any key rotation or identity migration that affects access to log storage during the requested window could erode trust unless we create an immutable snapshot.

Proposal (short-term exception + product asks):
1) Short-term exception: Create an \"evidence staging bucket\" (read-only) for acme-prod, populated with a signed bundle of audit logs compressed and checksumed (SHA256) prior to KMS rotation. This bundle is signed with current key material and the signature stored separately (key fingerprint + timestamp).
2) KMS rotation handling: Priya to perform a pre-rotation snapshot and export of relevant ciphertext and metadata. Post-rotation we keep an audit record (rotation manifest) and do not modify the exported bundle.
3) SSO migration mitigation: For the cutover weekend, ensure service accounts that write audit logs are excluded from any temporary deprovisioning; if that's not feasible, maintain a secondary writer to the staging bucket (read-only to Acme).
4) Product asks for Console: a minimal \"Exception ticket\" UI that records scope (tenant ID, time window), attachments (evidence bundle), approver (security + legal), and a tooling flag to mark the bundle immutable.

Action items (owners):
- Priya: confirm snapshot mechanism and whether we can sign/export prior to rotation (due 2027-06-18 EOD).
- Marcus: prototype export folder layout and checksum process; inject into Drive link for Legal (due 2027-06-19 noon).
- Jonas: draft exception language for Legal and Acme, include retention window and acceptance criteria (due 2027-06-18 5pm).
- Evelyn: coordinate CS / Customer success comms and request DocuSign if Acme needs a formal attestation (EOD).

If any of these are blocked, call it out in replies. Assuming Priya says snapshot is safe, we can commit to exception path and provide Acme the bundle + attestation before 06-29.

-Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Product Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, John Doe <john.doe@acmehealth.com>
Date: 2027-06-18T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window

Short technical note: snapshot + signing is feasible but with constraints.

What we can do now:
- We can perform a point-in-time export of the audit index and raw logs for tenant acme-prod. Export will include metadata (timestamps, request IDs), ciphertext blobs, and the current key fingerprint. We will compute a SHA256 checksum and use our current signing key (HSM-backed) to sign the bundle.

Risks & caveats:
- If rotation includes key deletion/decay policies that remove previous key material, we must escrow the key fingerprint + stored signature in a protected archive. We cannot provide plaintext keys.
- The export will be sufficient for an auditor to validate integrity if we include the rotation manifest and signature. But we need Legal to accept signature-of-bundle as equivalent to unbroken chain-of-custody.
- SSO migration risk: if service account credential churn during cutover prevents log writers from reaching GCS (our internal storage), we must temporarily route writes to the staging bucket or pause a limited set of operations. Pausing writes is undesirable; routing is preferred.

Questions for product & Legal:
- Is an HSM-signed bundle + rotation manifest acceptable as attestation?
- Do we need to provide any additional MAC or timestamp authority (e.g., RFC3161 timestamping)?

I'll start the export process tomorrow morning (2027-06-19 03:00 UTC) so we have the bundle ahead of rotation. If you want verbiage for Legal, I can add specific technical language to the exception doc.

-Priya
Priya Natarajan
Senior Staff, Infrastructure & Product Security
Redwood Inference


From: John Doe <john.doe@acmehealth.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com, audits@thirdparty.com
Date: 2027-06-19T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window

Hi all,

Thanks for the fast responses. Confirming the SOC2 window: auditor will begin evidence review 2027-07-01. Acme needs the export and a signed attestation that the logs are immutable and reflect production traffic for the period 2027-05-01 -> 2027-06-30.

A few clarifications from our side:
- The auditor will accept an HSM-signed bundle if Redwood's Legal signs off on the wording. We can mirror Redwood's template exception form if easier.
- We need a DocuSign-ready attestation (one-page) for our compliance folder.
- If there are any limitations to the export (e.g., missing fields due to SSO cutover), mark them in the attestation. Auditors appreciate transparency.

Please route the DocuSign link to procurement@acmehealth.com and copy audits@thirdparty.com.

Regards,
John Doe
Head of Security Programs
Acme Health


From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, John Doe <john.doe@acmehealth.com>, procurement@acmehealth.com
Date: 2027-06-21T17:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: KMS/SAML alignment for audit staging and retention window — decision and next steps

Folks — finalizing next steps based on triage:

Decision: we will grant a time-boxed exception and deliver an HSM-signed audit bundle for tenant acme-prod covering 2027-05-01 -> 2027-06-30. The bundle will be exported and signed prior to the KMS rotation (snapshot run 2027-06-19 03:00 UTC). Post-rotation we will not alter the exported artifacts.

Mitigations and scope:
- Priya will run the export and push the bundle to the evidence staging bucket with a rotation manifest. (Owner: Priya — done by 2027-06-19 12:00 PDT)
- Marcus will produce the checksum and put artifacts into the Drive folder and attach to the JIRA ticket SEC-9843. (Owner: Marcus — done by 2027-06-19 15:00 PDT)
- Marissa & I will draft the attestation language and send DocuSign to Acme procurement. Legal will review and add required boilerplate. (Owner: Jonas/Marissa — draft by 2027-06-18 17:00 PDT, Legal review by 2027-06-20)
- Evelyn to coordinate CS and confirm communications plan with Acme (Owner: Evelyn — notify Acme by 2027-06-20).

Open items / dependencies:
- Legal confirmation that HSM-signed bundle + rotation manifest satisfies chain-of-custody (Legal to confirm on SEC-9843).
- If the SSO cutover causes any write loss, that will be documented in the attestation. We'll keep Acme and their auditor informed.

I'll update SEC-9843 and copy Legal + CS. If anything changes (blocks or scope creep), escalate to me immediately.

Thanks all for the quick turnaround.

Jonas
Jonas Weber
Head of Security Partnerships
Redwood Inference

title: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@arborcloud.io>, Elena Ruiz <elena@arborcloud.io>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

Hi Samir / Elena,

Thanks again for the time last week — great to see the ArborCloud product team lined up for the pilot extension. Per our renewal window (contract ends 2027-07-31), we put together a short, pragmatic proposal that balances your need for predictable pricing during the Q3 migration and our capacity planning for the coming quarter. Attached is a one-page term summary and a draft term sheet (AV-ARBR-term-rebalance-v1.pdf).

High level (we can iterate):
- Term: 12 months starting 2027-08-01 with staged commit increases (see table in attachment): 0–90 days: current commit baseline; 91–180 days: +30% commit; 181–365 days: +60% commit.
- Temporary Price Guard: a 90-day fixed effective rate for the baseline model families used in your workload (applies to first tranche). This protects you from short-term market-driven price movement while we validate throughput during the migration.
- One-time onboarding credit: $25k credit applied as invoice offset if volume-based onboarding milestones hit in the first 60 days.
- True-up & monthly invoicing: monthly invoices with a quarterly true-up to reconcile usage against commit.

Questions for you:
1) Would ArborCloud accept staged commit triggers (activity-based) or prefer calendar-based tranches?
2) Procurement timing — do you need an executed PO before 2027-07-15 to meet your internal renewal window?

If this looks directionally right I can run the numbers with David (pricing) and Priya (CS/ops) and send a redline-ready copy for your legal/procurement. Happy to set a 30-min call tomorrow afternoon.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: AV-ARBR-term-rebalance-v1.pdf (term summary, draft schedule)


From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@arborcloud.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena@arborcloud.io>
Date: 2027-06-15T11:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

Avery — appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear option set. A few clarifying items from procurement / finance before we pull legal in: 

- Price guard scope: is the fixed rate limited to the baseline family only (we mainly use rwd-base and rwd-2b), or does it cover any model we run within the first 90 days? We need explicit families for our CAPEX forecasting.
- Trigger mechanics: activity-based triggers sound fair, but alerts and a simple dashboard view will be required so our SREs can track when tranches move. Can you include monitoring SLAs in the term sheet?
- Billing proration: if we sign 08/01 and actual usage ramps slower, will the first tranche be prorated or invoiced at commit with a subsequent credit?

I've attached our RFP workbook with the procurement timeline and PO contact (ArborCloud_RFP.xlsx). Procurement's deadline to have pricing locked is 2027-07-10. If we can get a final term sheet and a redline window this week, procurement will try to fast-track.

Cc'ing Elena from procurement — she will own the PO and contract logistics.

Thanks,
Samir Patel
Head of Platform Engineering | ArborCloud
samir.patel@arborcloud.io

Attachment: ArborCloud_RFP.xlsx (procurement timeline, PO contact)


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-16T09:32:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

Avery / Priya — looping on pricing mechanics. Quick notes after reviewing Samir's asks (quoted below).

> - Price guard scope: is the fixed rate limited to the baseline family only (we mainly use rwd-base and rwd-2b), or does it cover any model we run within the first 90 days?
> - Trigger mechanics: activity-based triggers sound fair...
> - Billing proration: if we sign 08/01 and actual usage ramps slower, will the first tranche be prorated or invoiced at commit with a subsequent credit?

Proposal from pricing side (draft; can soften):
- Price Guard: lock applies to explicitly listed model families: rwd-base and rwd-2b only (we can add rwd-lite as an exception). We will list families in the term sheet.
- Tranche pricing: 4% blended discount on the first tranche above baseline (applies during 0–90 days effective rate), 2% blended discount on subsequent tranches. Discounts are applied to per-token charges, not on fixed reserved fees.
- Proration: invoice at commit (no retro proration). If usage is below commit at the quarter true-up, we will apply a one-time credit up to 50% of the onboarding credit (so net downside protection) — this protects you while keeping clear economic signals.

If you want, I can draft exact table numbers (monthly commit, per-token rate, discount band) and a fallback clause for model substitution. Also recommend we add a simple dashboard requirement for tranche visibility (ops deliverable from our CS team).

—David
David Kim
Director, Pricing | Redwood Inference


From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>

Subject: Proposal: Stepwise Commit Rebalance & Temporary Price Guard

Hi Samir / Elena,

Thanks again for the time last week — great to see the ArborCloud product team lined up for the pilot extension. Per our renewal window (contract ends 2027-07-31), we put together a short, pragmatic proposal that balances your need for predictable pricing during the Q3 migration and our capacity planning for the coming quarter. Attached is a one-page term summary and a draft term sheet (AV-ARBR-term-rebalance-v1.pdf).

High level (we can iterate):
- Term: 12 months starting 2027-08-01 with staged commit increases (see table in attachment): 0–90 days: current commit baseline; 91–180 days: +30% commit; 181–365 days: +60% commit.
- Temporary Price Guard: a 90-day fixed effective rate for the baseline model families used in your workload (applies to first tranche). This protects you from short-term market-driven price movement while we validate throughput during the migration.
- One-time onboarding credit: $25k credit applied as invoice offset if volume-based onboarding milestones hit in the first 60 days.
- True-up & monthly invoicing: monthly invoices with a quarterly true-up to reconcile usage against commit.

Questions for you:
1) Would ArborCloud accept staged commit triggers (activity-based) or prefer calendar-based tranches?
2) Procurement timing — do you need an executed PO before 2027-07-15 to meet your internal renewal window?

If this looks directionally right I can run the numbers with David (pricing) and Priya (CS/ops) and send a redline-ready copy for your legal/procurement. Happy to set a 30-min call tomorrow afternoon.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson

title: Request: One-off integration engineering support + first-year credit for Sundial Health
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 13 Jun 2027 15:02:00 +0000
Subject: Sundial deal - request for one-off integration support + 30% first-year credit

Jonas / Connor —

Quick summary of Sundial Health (prospect -> fast close opportunity): they want concierge onboarding + a custom Hub-to-Redwood connector to map their clinical event stream (FHIR-lite). Procurement is pushing for no additional engineering fees and a single annual invoice. The commercial ask: include 40 hours of integration engineering (Redwood to build the Hub connector + run initial validation) at no charge, and apply a 30% credit to hosted inference usage for year one. In exchange they will sign a 12-month contract at the standard dedicated capacity tier and commit to a reference-case quote in our marketplace.

Attachments: Sundial_SOW_v2.pdf (redlined SOW), MVP-integration-specs.xlsx (scope + estimated hours).

Why we need deal desk/exec sign-off: the bundled free engineering work exceeds the standard onboarding allowance and the 30% credit impacts first-year ARR materially. Recommend we escalate to confirm we can grant a one-off concession and capture required approvals (legal T&Cs, finance credit memo).

Ask: Jonas — can you review and route for approval? Connor — please confirm the 40h estimate or update. Karthik — any immediate legal flags for a free-of-charge engineering component + modified billing cadence?

Thanks,
Priya
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
From: Priya Natarajan
(Onward quoting included attachments)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 13 Jun 2027 18:14:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sundial deal - request for one-off integration support + 30% first-year credit

Thanks Priya — reviewed the attached MVP spec. Quick notes:
- 40h is reasonable for a basic Hub connector (auth + event mapping + smoke tests) if Sundial provides a stable test dataset. If we need on-site or heavy data-cleaning, this could slip to ~80h.
- Risk: we will need a scoped sign-off on the schema mapping before dev starts; otherwise scope creep will eat hours. Recommend we include a simple acceptance test in the SOW (two sample flows validated).
- Post-launch support: I recommend we include an additional 8h of runbook/triage time (covered by premium support credits) rather than absorb ongoing ad-hoc requests indefinitely.

From a delivery standpoint I'm fine with the 40h concession as a one-off if we get exec OK and a tight SOW and acceptance criteria.

Connor
Principal Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Routing this to request approval for a one-time concession on the Sundial Health deal. Highlights:
- Customer: Sundial Health (fast-growing digital clinic, potential to expand across our telehealth partnerships)
- Commercial ask: 40h integration engineering included at no charge + 30% credit on hosted usage for the first 12 months
- Considerations: affects projected first-year revenue by ~29k (finance note in HubSpot), but increases chance of a quick close and potential marketplace/reference.

We need:
1) Legal: confirm we can keep a clause limiting free engineering to the 40h and require sign-off on acceptance tests; update SOW language to include a change-order process for additional work.
2) Finance/Revenue: sign-off on granting a 30% credit for year one and confirm whether we record as a contra-revenue or as a marketing credit per policy.
3) Exec: approval to proceed with the concession as-is or with modifications.

If we approve, next steps: update SOW, route to DocuSign, and finance will prepare a credit memo. Requested decision by EOD today to keep momentum.

Legal ok in principle, provided we:

- Include acceptance criteria and a sign-off form attached to the SOW.
- Limit the bundled engineering to non-sensitive data only and require Sundial to provide sanitized test data or an isolated sandbox account.

On the billing side, finance will need to approve the credit treatment. If we document the credit as a concession tied to execution milestones (50% on sign, 50% on acceptance), it will be easier to track.

Given the strategic value here and Connor's delivery containment suggestions, I support granting the concession with the above conditions.

title: Press asset & confidentiality — approval workflow and embargo timing
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@vertisys.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya.singh@vertisys.com>, Legal (Vertisys) <legal@vertisys.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 09:12:03 -0700
Subject: Draft order form redlines + publicity questions

Hannah —

Thanks for sending over the MSA and the order form yesterday. We reviewed the docs and have a couple of publicity-related asks before we finalize the order form:

- Embargo / announcement timing: we need a 30-day embargo from go-live for any joint press release. Can Redwood accommodate that?
- Logo usage: Vertisys requires a clause limiting logo use to one-line product mentions in press & case studies unless separate brand-asset approval is granted.
- Reference rights: our legal team prefers references to be by customer-approved quotes only; internal product screenshots would need a separate asset release.

I've attached our redline on the OrderForm (Vertisys_OrderForm_v3_redlines.pdf). Can your legal or contracts team confirm which items are negotiable? Also looping Maya (sales lead) so we can align on go-to-market timing.

Appreciate it —
Liam
Account Director, Vertisys
--- attachment: Vertisys_OrderForm_v3_redlines.pdf (pdf, 210 KB)


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@vertisys.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 11:03:22 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft order form redlines + publicity questions

Liam —

Thanks for the detailed redlines and the clear asks. Quick answers up front:

- Embargo: 30-day embargo is reasonable. We generally default to 14 days but can accept 30 for strategic accounts. We'll add the embargo language to the order form.
- Logo use: OK to restrict logo use to one-line product mentions without separate approval; for broader use (case studies, marketing assets) we'll require our standard asset approval flow.
- Reference rights: we can limit testimonials/quotes to customer-approved text. Screenshots will need asset clearance and a short review period.

Next steps I propose:
1) Priya (legal) to review Vertisys redlines and propose MSA/OrderForm language that maps to our standard publicity carveouts.
2) Ben (CS/Sales ops) to propose an operational sign-off checklist and ownership for asset approvals (who on Vertisys signs off, timing SLA).
3) Hannah to confirm final embargo date and any go-to-market windows once we have the contract language.

Can we aim for a legal read by Tuesday 2027-06-01 so Maya/Liam can lock launch planning?

Thanks —
Hannah
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@vertisys.com>, Maya Singh <maya.singh@vertisys.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 09:18:47 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft order form redlines + publicity questions

Team —

I've reviewed the Vertisys redlines and sketched suggested language. High-level notes:

- Embargo clause: proposed wording: "Customer and Redwood agree not to issue any public announcement relating to the Agreement, including press releases and joint marketing materials, until 30 days following Go-Live, unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing." This preserves flexibility for early case studies with express permission.

- Logo / brand use: proposed: "Redwood may use Customer's name and one-line product reference in marketing materials; any additional use of Customer logos, trademarks, or product screenshots requires prior written approval from Customer via the Asset Approval Process (see Exhibit X)."

- Asset Approval Process (Exhibit X): I drafted a pragmatic 5-step workflow including responsible approvers (Vertisys marketing lead + legal), max SLA for review (7 business days), and a template approval email. I can attach later as an exhibit or incorporate language directly into the Order Form.

Risks / ask: Vertisys redline removed our standard ability to publicly reference aggregate usage metrics; we should push to keep that high-level metric allowance (e.g., "Redwood may reference aggregate, anonymized usage statistics without Customer identification"). Please confirm if Vertisys is comfortable with that carveout.

I attached a short markup of the OrderForm and a draft Redwood Publicity Playbook (Redwood_Publicity_Playbook_draft.docx).

If everyone agrees, I will:
- Produce clean language for the order form and Exhibit X by EOD Monday 2027-05-31 (internal time),
- Send the package to Vertisys legal with tracked changes and a proposed sign-off checklist.

Priya
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Commercial
--- attachments: Redwood_Publicity_Playbook_draft.docx (docx, 145 KB)


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@vertisys.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft order form redlines + publicity questions

Thanks — this is helpful. We reviewed Priya's suggested wording with our legal and marketing teams. Short responses:

- Embargo 30 days: accepted.
- Logo / one-line mention + asset approval process: accepted; we like the 7-business-day SLA and the 5-step checklist.
- Aggregate usage metrics: Vertisys can accept anonymized, aggregate metrics so long as they do not identify Vertisys or reveal customer-specific data.

Action items before signing:
- Priya: please send the clean Order Form language + Exhibit X for our legal redline.
- Ben: can you draft the sign-off matrix naming Vertisys approvers (we'll provide contacts) and the internal Redwood reviewer list?
- Hannah & Maya: coordinate go-to-market calendar once contract is final — launch window tentatively set for 2027-07-15 to 2027-07-29 (no public comms before 2027-07-15).

If possible, can we get the clean doc by tomorrow morning (2027-06-02) PST to keep the timeline?

Best,
Liam
Account Director, Vertisys
