title: Clarification: incident notification timing & evidence access for GreenField Health
preview:
From: Anna Schultz <anna.schultz@greenfieldhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: GreenField Health Legal <legal@greenfieldhealth.com>; Marco Esposito <marco.esposito@greenfieldhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal team is reviewing the DPA and we hit a couple of points we need clarified before we move to signature. Two things in particular:

1) Notification timing: the DPA references \"prompt notice\" and then an internal SLA of 72 hours for \"material\" incidents. Can you confirm whether Redwood can commit to an initial notification within 48 hours for incidents affecting PHI (our baseline)? If not, what operational steps might shorten the time to first meaningful update?

2) Evidence access & export: our security team needs to understand how forensic evidence (telemetry, ingress logs, snapshots) can be accessed. The DPA has a broad \"cooperation\" clause but is silent on cross-border transfer for raw logs. We may require either (a) a controlled transfer to our EU-based forensic partner, (b) an on-prem evidence escrow, or (c) remote, read-only forensic sessions run by our vendor under supervision. Which options does Redwood support?

Attached is the excerpt of the clauses we are looking at. Can your team confirm operationally what is feasible and whether any wording changes are needed?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — aiming for signature by end of month.

Best,
Anna
Director, InfoSec
GreenField Health
[attachment: gf-dpa-excerpt.pdf (pdf)]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Anna Schultz <anna.schultz@greenfieldhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Hi Anna — thanks for the clear note and the excerpt. Looping in Ben (sales lead) and Priya (privacy/ops) to confirm operational details. High level answers below, follow-ups from Priya on technical controls: 

1) Notification timing: our standard SLA for \"material\" incidents today is initial customer outreach within 72 hours with a preliminary impact assessment. We have an internal fast-path for PHI or regulatory incidents that typically yields a same-day acknowledgement and a 36–48 hour preliminary assessment (status + scope). We cannot legally guarantee 48 hours for every PHI incident in the standard DPA text today, but we can propose a contractual commitment to: initial acknowledgement within 48 hours for incidents classified as involving regulated data (PHI/PCI/Personal identities) followed by a 72-hour preliminary assessment deliverable. That would be a limited amendment to Section 5.2 (Notification) — I can draft suggested language.

2) Evidence access & export: we support two operational patterns commonly used with customers:
- Controlled remote sessions: our security team can provide time-boxed, read-only remote forensic sessions (VPN + jumpbox) where customer or customer-appointed forensics runs queries against logs.
- Secure forensic export: we can produce sanitized forensic exports under NDA and using approved transfer mechanisms. Cross-border exports are permitted but may require data minimization or pseudonymization depending on origin.

We do not currently do on-prem evidence escrow as a standard offering, though we can explore a one-off arrangement with legal and engineering (it has cost/ops implications).

Priya — can you confirm the typical timeline for producing a forensic snapshot and whether we have an SOP for EU-local partner handoffs (e.g., remote-only vs. file export)?

Chloe
Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (pdf)]

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Chloe / Ben — internal ops detail below so we can provide a precise proposal to GreenField: 

- Initial ack / triage: for incidents flagged as \"regulated\" (PHI/PCI), security triage will produce an initial acknowledgement within 24 hours business hours and a scoped preliminary assessment within 48–72 hours. This fits with Chloe's proposed contract language (48-hour ack + 72-hour assessment).

- Forensic snapshot: Runtimes + telemetry snapshot (KV dumps, request headers, minimal payload sampling) can typically be produced in 7–10 business days for full export. We can produce an abbreviated evidence package (logs + IOCs + sampling) in 48–72 hours when prioritized, but that requires a statement of scope and approval from legal/security to avoid over-exporting production data.

- Cross-border transfers: we have two supported patterns: (1) remote read-only sessions hosted in the data region (preferred for EU customers), and (2) encrypted export to a customer-designated S3 endpoint subject to export checklist and minimization. We will not do unrestricted raw data dumps to external parties without a signed Data Processing Amendment addendum + Destination Transfer Addendum.

- One-off escrow/on-prem: doable but treated as a professional services engagement (engineering time + security review + additional contract terms).

Action items we should propose to GreenField in our reply: (A) Proposed DPA text that commits to 48-hour acknowledgement for regulated incidents; (B) Offer remote read-only forensic sessions as default; (C) Add export process language allowing encrypted, scoped transfers to customer endpoints with pre-approved export checklist; (D) Escrow/on-prem as PS option.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Privacy & Trust Operations
Redwood Inference
[attachment: forensic-snapshot-req-template.docx (docx)]

From: Anna Schultz <anna.schultz@greenfieldhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: GreenField Health Legal <legal@greenfieldhealth.com>, Marco Esposito <marco.esposito@greenfieldhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Chloe / Priya / Ben — this is helpful, thanks. Our GC wants explicit wording for the following items to be added to the DPA before sign: 
1) A clause committing to \"initial acknowledgement within 48 hours\" for incidents that Customer-designates as involving regulated data (PHI/PCI), with a 72-hour preliminary assessment deliverable and a 30-day forensic report timeline unless otherwise agreed.
2) A description of the evidence access model: default to remote, read-only forensic sessions hosted in the same data region; exports permitted only via encrypted, scoped transfer to customer-controlled endpoints following an Export Checklist; exports to third-party vendors require written consent and a Data Transfer Addendum.
3) A line about subprocessors: Redwood will notify Customer of any subprocessor used in evidence handling and will ensure contractual obligations consistent with the DPA.

If you can provide a tracked-change DPA addendum with the wording above by EOD Monday we can wrap this up quickly. Also forward any cost estimate for an on-prem escrow option, since that may be requested by procurement.

Best,
Anna

text:
From: Anna Schultz <anna.schultz@greenfieldhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: GreenField Health Legal <legal@greenfieldhealth.com>; Marco Esposito <marco.esposito@greenfieldhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal team is reviewing the DPA and we hit a couple of points we need clarified before we move to signature. Two things in particular:

1) Notification timing: the DPA references \"prompt notice\" and then an internal SLA of 72 hours for \"material\" incidents. Can you confirm whether Redwood can commit to an initial notification within 48 hours for incidents affecting PHI (our baseline)? If not, what operational steps might shorten the time to first meaningful update?

2) Evidence access & export: our security team needs to understand how forensic evidence (telemetry, ingress logs, snapshots) can be accessed. The DPA has a broad \"cooperation\" clause but is silent on cross-border transfer for raw logs. We may require either (a) a controlled transfer to our EU-based forensic partner, (b) an on-prem evidence escrow, or (c) remote, read-only forensic sessions run by our vendor under supervision. Which options does Redwood support?

Attached is the excerpt of the clauses we are looking at. Can your team confirm operationally what is feasible and whether any wording changes are needed?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — aiming for signature by end of month.

Best,
Anna
Director, InfoSec
GreenField Health
[attachment: gf-dpa-excerpt.pdf (pdf)]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Anna Schultz <anna.schultz@greenfieldhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Hi Anna — thanks for the clear note and the excerpt. Looping in Ben (sales lead) and Priya (privacy/ops) to confirm operational details. High level answers below, follow-ups from Priya on technical controls: 

1) Notification timing: our standard SLA for \"material\" incidents today is initial customer outreach within 72 hours with a preliminary impact assessment. We have an internal fast-path for PHI or regulatory incidents that typically yields a same-day acknowledgement and a 36–48 hour preliminary assessment (status + scope). We cannot legally guarantee 48 hours for every PHI incident in the standard DPA text today, but we can propose a contractual commitment to: initial acknowledgement within 48 hours for incidents classified as involving regulated data (PHI/PCI/Personal identities) followed by a 72-hour preliminary assessment deliverable. That would be a limited amendment to Section 5.2 (Notification) — I can draft suggested language.

2) Evidence access & export: we support two operational patterns commonly used with customers:
- Controlled remote sessions: our security team can provide time-boxed, read-only remote forensic sessions (VPN + jumpbox) where customer or customer-appointed forensics runs queries against logs.
- Secure forensic export: we can produce sanitized forensic exports under NDA and using approved transfer mechanisms. Cross-border exports are permitted but may require data minimization or pseudonymization depending on origin.

We do not currently do on-prem evidence escrow as a standard offering, though we can explore a one-off arrangement with legal and engineering (it has cost/ops implications).

Priya — can you confirm the typical timeline for producing a forensic snapshot and whether we have an SOP for EU-local partner handoffs (e.g., remote-only vs. file export)?

Chloe
Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (pdf)]

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Chloe / Ben — internal ops detail below so we can provide a precise proposal to GreenField: 

- Initial ack / triage: for incidents flagged as \"regulated\" (PHI/PCI), security triage will produce an initial acknowledgement within 24 hours business hours and a scoped preliminary assessment within 48–72 hours. This fits with Chloe's proposed contract language (48-hour ack + 72-hour assessment).

- Forensic snapshot: Runtimes + telemetry snapshot (KV dumps, request headers, minimal payload sampling) can typically be produced in 7–10 business days for full export. We can produce an abbreviated evidence package (logs + IOCs + sampling) in 48–72 hours when prioritized, but that requires a statement of scope and approval from legal/security to avoid over-exporting production data.

- Cross-border transfers: we have two supported patterns: (1) remote read-only sessions hosted in the data region (preferred for EU customers), and (2) encrypted export to a customer-designated S3 endpoint subject to export checklist and minimization. We will not do unrestricted raw data dumps to external parties without a signed Data Processing Amendment addendum + Destination Transfer Addendum.

- One-off escrow/on-prem: doable but treated as a professional services engagement (engineering time + security review + additional contract terms).

Action items we should propose to GreenField in our reply: (A) Proposed DPA text that commits to 48-hour acknowledgement for regulated incidents; (B) Offer remote read-only forensic sessions as default; (C) Add export process language allowing encrypted, scoped transfers to customer endpoints with pre-approved export checklist; (D) Escrow/on-prem as PS option.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Privacy & Trust Operations
Redwood Inference
[attachment: forensic-snapshot-req-template.docx (docx)]

Cc: GreenField Health Legal <legal@greenfieldhealth.com>, Marco Esposito <marco.esposito@greenfieldhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: contract questions — incident notification window and evidence access

Chloe / Priya / Ben — this is helpful, thanks. Our GC wants explicit wording for the following items to be added to the DPA before sign: 
1) A clause committing to \"initial acknowledgement within 48 hours\" for incidents that Customer-designates as involving regulated data (PHI/PCI), with a 72-hour preliminary assessment deliverable and a 30-day forensic report timeline unless otherwise agreed.
2) A description of the evidence access model: default to remote, read-only forensic sessions hosted in the same data region; exports permitted only via encrypted, scoped transfer to customer-controlled endpoints following an Export Checklist; exports to third-party vendors require written consent and a Data Transfer Addendum.
3) A line about subprocessors: Redwood will notify Customer of any subprocessor used in evidence handling and will ensure contractual obligations consistent with the DPA.

If you can provide a tracked-change DPA addendum with the wording above by EOD Monday we can wrap this up quickly. Also forward any cost estimate for an on-prem escrow option, since that may be requested by procurement.

title: Committed compute path & isolation — post-call recap
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lara Chen <lara.chen@orbitalbio.com>, Procurement <procurement@orbitalbio.com>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Committed compute path & isolation — post-call recap

Thanks again for a productive call today — appreciate the clear description of your latency sensitivity and regulatory constraints. Quick recap and the next steps we agreed on, plus a couple of follow-ups from our side.

High-level summary (pain / goals)
- Pain: model inference variability on burst loads and noisy multi-tenant interference; current infra shows 2-3x latency spikes at peak.
- Goal: predictable token-level latency for customer-facing chat (target p95 < 150ms at steady state for 200 concurrent users; peak scaling to ~1k rps).
- Compliance: PHI-adjacent workflows require data-plane isolation and customer-controlled KMS for keys.

What we proposed (committed pool profile)
- Committed GPU pool (isolated tenant lane) with 8x A100-similar V100-equivalent slots to start; autoscaling headroom + burst buffer to handle short 10–20% traffic bursts.
- Isolation: private VPC peering, separate node pool, and optional on-prem connector for sealed workloads.
- SLA targets we iterated: 99.95% uptime for the pool; p95 generation latency target of < 150ms under provisioned throughput.

Technical constraints / assumptions from your side
- Model family: medium-sized decoder-only models (estimated 16–20B parameters) with 512 token windows.
- Expected Q3 peak: 200k tokens/day ramping to 900k in six months.

Immediate next steps (owner / ETA)
- Redwood: share a short committed-pool outline (attached) + preliminary SOW for pricing anchors — Ben (today / EOD).
- Orbital: confirm procurement contact and whether SOC2 artifact bundle is needed up-front — Lara (by Fri 5/21).
- Schedule a 60-minute technical deep-dive with infra and security to validate KV-cache behavior and network topology — propose Tue 5/25 10:00–11:00 PT.

Attachments: redwood-committed-pool-outline-v1.pdf (capacity profile, isolation options), poa-action-items.xlsx (simple checklist)

If anything above looks off or you want different SLA/throughput targets called out explicitly, say the word and we’ll adjust. Otherwise I’ll circulate the SOW anchor and calendar invite for the deep-dive.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182

---

Carla / team —

Thanks for sending — I reviewed with Evelyn and Karthik. High-level proposed changes we put together (please see our attached redline RFQ_Redline_ConnorProspect_v1.docx):

- Quiet period: propose a compromise of a 14-day quiet period tied to the first public announcement date, not the internal launch. This keeps momentum for joint comms while giving both teams time to prep.

- Logo usage: request a small technical appendix limiting logo modifications and specifying proper trademark attribution.
- Confidentiality carve-out: add a narrow carve-out that permits Redwood to reference the customer in non-sales internal reporting.

- Can Redwood accept 21 days instead of 14? It gives our comms team time for approvals across regions.
- For the "pre-approval within 5 business days" language, please specify the approval owner (example: "Customer's marketing director or legal may approve"). It helps avoid back-and-forth.

Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — quiet-period language & brand acknowledgment

Carla —

Appreciate the context and the flexibility. Redwood can accept a 21-day quiet period tied to the public announcement date. For approvals, we'll add the following language: "Customer's marketing director or designated communications lead shall have 5 business days to approve marketing collateral; if no response is provided within that window, approval will be deemed granted unless reasonable objections are raised in writing."

Approval flow we will follow:
- Redwood creates draft copy and logo usage mockups (Product Marketing).
- Customer marketing director (named approver) reviews within 5 business days.

title: Request: Summer compute supplement for Data Curation Consortium
preview:
From: Dr. Hannah Li <hannah.li@midwest.edu>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:22:00 -0400
Subject: Request: Summer compute supplement for Data Curation Consortium

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the pilot credits you provided earlier this year — the team at the Midwest University Research Consortium (MURC) has been able to validate the data curation pipeline on ~3.2M tokens. We’ve now landed a small NSF-funded extension to finish annotation for the remaining 1.1M tokens, but our institutional procurement timeline means we can’t get a PO in place before the work window closes (mid-June).

Would Redwood be able to provide a short-term supplement (trial/top-up) — roughly $5k of credits or equivalent token allocation — so we can complete the curated dataset and reproduce results for the final paper? We’re a non-profit academic consortium; the work is non-commercial and will be released under an open dataset license. We attached a one-page scope and an estimated usage spreadsheet.

If you need a confirmation letter from the PI or a short MOU, we can provide that quickly. Appreciate any flexibility — happy to hop on a 20-min call if helpful.

Best,
Hannah
Dr. Hannah Li
Research Director, MURC
Midwest University

Attachments:
- consortium_scope.pdf (one-page scope)
- usage-projections.xlsx (estimated token usage and researcher list)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

- Would you be able to accept a short-term promo credit plus a simple MOU (we can use our template) that states the dataset is non-commercial and open?

If those answers look good I’ll check with my manager for discretionary support and loop in Priya on CS for onboarding/usage guardrails. If it helps I can also propose a conditional supplement (e.g., $5k credit valid for 60 days) to cover the June window.

Best,
Ben Carter

From: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>

Subject: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — quick follow up from our finance team. We signed the annual commitment last quarter but are implementing an internal escrow split across our UK and NL entities. Before we finalize the PO and wiring, can you confirm:  

- How Redwood intends to schedule the committed-spend sweep windows (monthly / quarterly), and whether we can request non-standard timing for the first two tranches.
- Whether Redwood can issue split invoices to two legal entities (Bayside UK Ltd and Bayside NL BV) for the same commitment, and how VAT will be applied on those invoices.

Our treasury team also asks for a sample invoice and the exact bank details you will use for a split wiring. I’ve attached our internal ledger notes (no PII) for context.

Appreciate a short timeline — we need this confirmed to pull the PO through our S2P system by next Wednesday.

Thanks,
Marisol
Marisol Vega
Head of Finance | Bayside Analytics
marisol.vega@baysideanalytics.com

Attachment: bayside-ledger-notes.csv (CSV stub)

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 10:02:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split

Hi Marisol — thanks for the clear ask. Short answers and a suggested plan below; I’ll loop in billing/tax for the final confirmation.

Proposed plan (can do non-standard timing for first two tranches):
- Sweep cadence: Default monthly sweep on the 1st unless you opt for quarterly. For this customer we can schedule the first two tranches on 2027-05-15 and 2027-06-15 to align with your payroll.
- Split invoices: We support issuing invoices to multiple legal entities associated with a single commitment, but we must map the committed amount to entity-level ledger codes up front.
- VAT treatment: If the services are taxed in the buyer country, we’ll apply VAT to the invoice issued by the corresponding Redwood legal entity. If you want reverse-charge handling (B2B EU), we need each entity’s VAT registration numbers and a signed VAT attestation.

What I’m attaching: a proposed invoice-schedule CSV (shows entity split and dates) and a draft sample invoice for review.

Next steps I need from you by Tue EOD:  
1) Confirm which two legal entities will receive invoices (exact legal names + VAT IDs).
2) Confirm preferred sweep dates for the first two tranches (default above unless you tell us otherwise).
3) PO number and beneficiary details if the wiring will be split.

I’ll open an internal ticket with Billing for bank details and the final invoice routing.

Cheers,
Ethan Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.com
Attachment: proposed-invoice-schedule.csv; invoice-draft-Bayside-RWD-893.pdf

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 09:05:00 +0100
Subject: Fwd: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split — need billing/tax confirm

Kimberly / Priya — forwarding Bayside’s note and our proposed schedule. They want: split invoices to two legal entities (UK + NL), non-standard first two sweep dates (15 May and 15 June), and sample invoice + bank details.

Key questions:  
- Can Redwood invoice two entities for the same annual commitment while preserving a single committed balance and drawdown tracking? If so, how do we reflect that in the ledger (one commit id, two invoice refs)?
- VAT matrix: for UK-issued invoices do we charge UK VAT, and for NL invoices do we apply Dutch VAT or reverse-charge? See attached customer VAT IDs (from their reply).
- Any constraints on split wiring (partial wires to different Redwood bank accounts) — treasury needs the final remit instructions.

I attached: BAY_LEDGER_NOTES.csv (from customer), proposed-invoice-schedule.csv, draft invoice. Can you confirm by EOD Tuesday so I can reply to Marisol and keep their PO timeline?

—Ethan

---- Original Message ----
From: Marisol Vega <mvega@baysideanalytics.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 09:12:00 +0100
Subject: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>

(quoted above)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 11:18:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Fwd: Clarify sweep windows & VAT treatment for escrow split — need billing/tax confirm

Thanks — reviewed. High level notes and operational asks:  

1) Committed balance / ledger: We can keep a single commitment id (RWD-2024-893) and issue multiple invoice documents against that commit. Each invoice will reference the commit id and have its own invoice number. Accounting will net the receipts against the commit balance. Please ensure the invoice schedule CSV has an explicit column for commit id and entity invoice_ref.

2) VAT routing rules (per our tax counsel):
- If Redwood issues an invoice from Redwood EU Ltd (NL) to Bayside NL BV and the buyer is VAT-registered, we apply zero-rated B2B reverse-charge if the service qualifies — buyer must provide VAT number and sign the attestation.
- UK invoices: post-Brexit, services invoiced from our UK legal entity will be subject to UK VAT where place-of-supply rules indicate. If Bayside UK is VAT-registered, we can zero-rate with reverse-charge but we need their VAT certificate.

3) Split wiring / bank accounts: We can accept split wires, but each invoice must include the precise remit instructions and bank account. Treasury prefers a single collection then internal allocation; if Bayside insists on two separate wires we will provide two account refs.

Attachments I uploaded: vat-matrix-bayside.xlsx (mapping rules), vat-routing-guidance.docx (short legal note), invoice-draft-Bayside-RWD-893.pdf (updated with invoice_ref columns).

Operational steps for Ethan to confirm to customer:
- Provide legal entity names + VAT IDs for both Bayside entities.
- Confirm preferred first two sweep dates: 2027-05-15 and 2027-06-15 (or propose alternatives).
- Indicate if they want a single wired amount to Redwood with Redwood performing allocation, or two separate wires.

I’ll queue the invoices once we have the VAT docs and PO.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Billing & Revenue Ops, Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com

Attachment: vat-matrix-bayside.xlsx; vat-routing-guidance.docx

Plan we can execute once we receive the documents below:  
- Sweep dates (agreed): 2027-05-15 and 2027-06-15 for the first two tranches, monthly thereafter on the 1st.
- Invoice routing: Redwood will issue two invoices (one to Bayside UK Ltd, one to Bayside NL BV). Each invoice will reference commit id RWD-2024-893 and have a separate invoice number for your AP system.
- VAT: We will follow the guidance Kimberly attached. For NL issuance we will apply reverse-charge conditional on Bayside NL providing their VAT registration and signed attestation. 3) Preferred wiring option (A or B).

To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>

Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — GlyphData is excited to run a two-month pilot with Redwood for our inference-backed analytics. Procurement asked me to confirm whether we can be onboarded on an invoice-only basis for the first 90 days (no PO). Our ERP takes time to provision new vendor IDs, and we'd like to start billing sooner.

Attached is the draft SOW and a sample invoice format procurement wants to use. Can Redwood accept invoice-only for a pilot under $12k total, with a short-term W-9 and insurance cert on file?

Sincerely,
Maya Patel
Head of Data Partnerships
GlyphData Inc.

Attachments: GlyphData_SOW_v1.pdf (pdf), pilot_invoice_sample.xlsx (xlsx)

To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Team — forwarding GlyphData inquiry. Key asks: invoice-only for 90 days, pilot cap ~$12k, W-9 and insurance provided.

Quick asks:
- Priya: Is this eligible for our temporary invoice-only path? Any red flags on insurance/W-9?
- Marcus: If we allow invoice-only, what guardrails (thresholds, payment terms, collections) do we want?
- Laura: Finance — approve temporary exception subject to controls?

I think pilot looks low-risk, but want formal sign-off before confirming.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Alex / team —

Quick checklist for an invoice-only pilot (based on current playbook):
1) Pilot cap: <= $15,000 (one-off) — ok to approve without PO if other conditions met.
2) Required evidence before start: signed SOW, W-9, COI (insurance).
3) Payment terms: Net 30; require procurement contact and billing email.
4) Risk controls: tag account as "invoice-only pilot" in ERP; 30-day internal review; no auto-renew without PO.
5) Collections: finance to send reminder at 45 days; if unpaid at 60 days, suspend access.

Notes/Questions:
- Insurance: the COI template must show general liability and cyber coverage >= $1M.
- Currency/Tax: assume USD; if GlyphData requires foreign tax docs, escalate.

If Glyph provides the docs and SOW is within the cap, we can move forward. Marcus, Laura — any objections?

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Vendor Intake & Procurement
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 10:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Agree with Priya's checklist. A couple of additions from ops perspective:
- Threshold: recommend we set the operational threshold at $12,500 for a single pilot (to match their ask and leave headroom).
- Access gating: create a temporary API key with a quota cap (500k tokens / month) tied to the pilot SOW.
- Billing: require procurement billing contact in the SOW and a valid email to receive invoices; use explicit invoice subject including "Pilot — GlyphData — Redwood" to avoid routing delays.
- Escalation: if invoice not paid within 45 days, ops should suspend the API key; collections to cc account owner and procurement team.

I'll prepare the short form exception approval packet for Laura to sign.

Marcus Lin
Platform Operations
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 14:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

All — approved with conditions below.
1) Pilot cap approved at $12,500 total.
2) Required docs (signed SOW, W-9, COI with required coverage) must be uploaded to vendor profile before invoices are issued.
3) Net 30 billing; finance to send 2 reminders at 30 and 45 days, after which access will be suspended.
4) No automatic conversion to paid subscription. A PO and legal review required for any renewal or scale beyond $12.5k.

Alex — please confirm to GlyphData and request procurement to send the W-9 and COI. Marcus/Priya — I'll sign the exception packet once you attach it to the ticket.

Laura Bennett
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>

Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Alex / team —

Quick checklist for an invoice-only pilot (based on current playbook):
1) Pilot cap: <= $15,000 (one-off) — ok to approve without PO if other conditions met.
2) Required evidence before start: signed SOW, W-9, COI (insurance).
3) Payment terms: Net 30; require procurement contact and billing email.
4) Risk controls: tag account as "invoice-only pilot" in ERP; 30-day internal review; no auto-renew without PO.
5) Collections: finance to send reminder at 45 days; if unpaid at 60 days, suspend access.

Notes/Questions:
- Insurance: the COI template must show general liability and cyber coverage >= $1M.
- Currency/Tax: assume USD; if GlyphData requires foreign tax docs, escalate.

If Glyph provides the docs and SOW is within the cap, we can move forward. Marcus, Laura — any objections?

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Vendor Intake & Procurement
Redwood Inference

Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 10:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Agree with Priya's checklist. A couple of additions from ops perspective:
- Threshold: recommend we set the operational threshold at $12,500 for a single pilot (to match their ask and leave headroom).
- Access gating: create a temporary API key with a quota cap (500k tokens / month) tied to the pilot SOW.
- Billing: require procurement billing contact in the SOW and a valid email to receive invoices; use explicit invoice subject including "Pilot — GlyphData — Redwood" to avoid routing delays.
- Escalation: if invoice not paid within 45 days, ops should suspend the API key; collections to cc account owner and procurement team.

I'll prepare the short form exception approval packet for Laura to sign.

Marcus Lin
Platform Operations
Redwood Inference

3) Net 30 billing; finance to send 2 reminders at 30 and 45 days, after which access will be suspended.
4) No automatic conversion to paid subscription. A PO and legal review required for any renewal or scale beyond $12.5k.

Cc: procurement@brightline.io

Subject: Quick question on PO requirement for pilot

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team loved the low-latency routing examples. We’re lined up to start a 6-week technical pilot next Monday but hit a snag with our procurement team: our accounts payable process requires a signed PO for any vendor onboarding.

Because this is a short technical pilot and we’ve agreed to capped spend (~$6k), procurement is asking whether Redwood can accept invoice-only terms for the pilot period and provide some form of evidence/certification (SOC2 attestation, data flow diagram) for their record.

Can you confirm whether Redwood supports a temporary invoice-only onboarding for new pilots, and what evidence you can provide? If needed I can loop in our contracts person.

Attaching our standard onboarding form for your intake team (Brightline_Onboarding_Form_v1.pdf).

Appreciate a quick note — we need the procurement sign-off by EOD tomorrow to keep the Monday start.

Best,
Marta Ruiz
Head of IT Procurement
Brightline Labs

To: Marta Ruiz <marta@brightline.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.example.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.example.com>
Date: 2027-04-26T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on PO requirement for pilot

Hi Marta — thanks for the heads up and for sharing the onboarding form.

Short answer: we can support a temporary invoice-only onboarding for pilots under a controlled exception, provided Finance signs off and we collect a minimal set of compliance artifacts.

What we need from Brightline to proceed (quick checklist):
- Signed pilot statement of work or MOU (term, spend cap, duration) — a simple DocuSign is fine.
- SOC 2 report OR link to your vendor security page + point of contact (if SOC2 not available).
- Completed onboarding form (you already shared this — thanks).
- Primary billing contact and PO/Invoice routing email.

Operational constraints from our side: temporary exception limited to one invoice-only window for the pilot (max $10k). After pilot we’ll require PO for ongoing purchases. The exception will be recorded in our procurement tracker and a brief finance approval XLS (attached) will be created.

I’m looping in Priya (Sales Ops) to help collect the onboarding answers and Naomi in Finance to request the formal exception. Priya — can your team intake Brightline_Onboarding_Form_v1.pdf into the vendor portal and confirm the missing fields by 3pm PT? Naomi — any initial red flags on the $6k pilot? If you’re okay I’ll ask Marta for the MOU link and Bill-to contact.

Next steps:
1) Priya to confirm intake checklist items.
2) Brightline to send MOU link or DocuSign.
3) Naomi to confirm temporary exception approval (email is fine).

-Ben

Redwood Inference

From: Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com>

Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We finished a brief internal technical review and have one request before we sign: IvyWorks needs a narrowly scoped availability commitment for the /chat/generate endpoint during US business hours (M-F, 8am-6pm PT). Specifically we want a contractual p95 response-time commitment at or below 80ms for our production key path for the first 12 months.

We understand this is outside standard terms — we're open to a higher-tier Reserved / Dedicated plan and a short ramp schedule. Can you outline what Redwood would need to agree to operationally and in contract terms? I've attached our SLO spreadsheet and a one-page mutual action plan.

Best,
Emma
CTO, IvyWorks

Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Team — forwarding IvyWorks' ask. Quick summary: customer wants a targeted p95 <= 80ms for /chat/generate during US business hours for 12 months. They're willing to go to Dedicated and accept a ramp. Legal (Priya) will need to weigh in on risk language. Ops/Runtime (Marcus) — can we provide guidance today on whether this is feasible, and what guardrails / runbook items we would require? CS (Grace) — can you add expected QPS and concurrency + any historical traffic patterns?

Goal: have a short internal decision (yes/no + required conditions) by EOD tomorrow so I can respond to Emma with a proposed path (Dedicated capacity + warm-up + canary + formal runbook).

> On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 9:02 AM Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com> wrote:

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 14:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Short answer: doable with constraints. Technical notes below — high level first:

- Feasible path: Dedicated GPU pool pinned to their tenant for business-hours traffic + pre-warmed KV cache + request routing to low-latency nodes in us-west region. Expect realistic steady-state p95 ~60-90ms for small-gen prompts if we (1) reserve capacity for peak concurrency they expect and (2) prevent cold-starts during hours.
- Hard guardrails: we need a clearly defined traffic envelope (QPS, concurrency, prompt length distribution). If they spike outside agreed envelope, commitment pauses until manual approval.
- Operational asks:
  * Pre-warm schedule + synthetic load test during canary
  * Dedicated monitoring dashboard + alerting thresholds for p95 and queue length
  * Incident response SLA for paging on real p95 breach (who to oncall)
  * A fallback model variant with broader latency but lower cost (automated fallback)

Attachments: rough ops runbook sketch attached. If legal requires a monetary SLA credit we need to size it against committed reserved capacity rather than per-token.

I'll need CS numbers from Grace to give capacity sizing. Also: we should avoid blanket "sub-80ms for all endpoints" language — make it specific to /chat/generate with defined max tokens and input size.

- Marcus
Engineering — Serving Runtime

[Attachment: proposed-ops-runbook.md (text/markdown)]

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks — adding customer context. IvyWorks is a strategic SMB from our Q1 book; current daily usage is ~30k tokens/day with a predictable diurnal pattern. Expected ramp: 2-3x over 6 weeks post-launch. Peak concurrency during business hours hits ~18 concurrent sessions today. Average prompt length: 40 tokens. They are adamant about p95 for the specific path they showed in the demo (short prompt, single-turn).

Notes for negotiation: they prefer a single-line SLO in the contract rather than a long appendix. That makes our engineering ask of a narrowly-scoped SLO even more important. Suggest we offer:
  - Commitment: p95 <= 85ms for /chat/generate (max input tokens 64, max output tokens 128) during M-F 8am-6pm PT
  - Preconditions: Dedicated capacity reservation (3x current concurrency as initial commit) + 7-day warm-up with synthetic traffic + agreed-upon ramp cap and an automatic throttle if envelope exceeded
  - Measurement: Redwood's telemetry on the dedicated pool; daily digests to IvyWorks and a monthly compliance report

If legal is okay with language around "service credit proportional to reserved capacity for verifiable breaches", I think we can close quickly.

— Grace
Customer Success


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks all — flagged legal considerations below. High-level: we can accept a narrowly-scoped availability/latency commitment if the following appear in the contract:

1) Explicit scope definition (endpoint, token limits, business hours).
2) Traffic envelope and failure modes: a clause that excludes outages caused by customer-initiated traffic above the envelope, abuse, or third-party network failures.
3) Limited SLA credit: define credits as percentage of monthly reserved fee (caps + tiers) rather than per-token.
4) Exit & review cadence: 90-day operational review clause to evaluate the SLO post-ramp; right to adjust terms if we see abuse or unanticipated costs.
5) Auditability: Redwood's telemetry will be the source of truth; provide sample report format to customer.

If product and engineering approve the operational guardrails Marcus outlined, legal can draft a short SLO addendum and redline the MAP. I recommend we avoid open-ended language like "guaranteed sub-80ms" without the other constraints.

I'll prepare a first-pass SLO addendum draft once Marcus confirms capacity sizing and Grace confirms QPS ramp.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: Onward transfer authorization request — vendor roster & SCCs
preview:
From: Amrita Shah <amrita.shah@healthplus.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Legal Ops (HealthPlus) <legal@healthplus.com>
Date: 2027-04-18 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor roster + onward transfer permissions for Upstream integration

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Legal is doing our due diligence before we enable the Upstream connector for production. Two quick asks so we can move forward with procurement:

1) Current vendor roster for any downstream processors (a flat CSV is ideal) including country of processing, purpose, and whether onward transfers are permitted.
2) Confirmation that Redwood will accept SCCs (or equivalent) for transfers to the EU, and whether you require any additional delegation for Upstream (they will host logs for 30 days).

Could you send the roster and any signed SCC artifacts or guidance on what we need to sign? If you prefer, loop in your privacy or security lead.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — we’re targeting a production go-live 05/10.

Best,
Amrita
Amrita Shah
Senior Procurement Manager, HealthPlus


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Amrita Shah <amrita.shah@healthplus.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-18 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor roster + onward transfer permissions for Upstream integration

Hi Amrita —

Thanks for the note and the timeline — we can get you what you need. High-level answers up front:
- We accept SCCs for EU transfers and can provide a signed copy for the relevant processor chain.
- Upstream will be treated as a downstream processor for logging and telemetry; we can provide the specific scope and a short DPIA summary for their connector.

I’m attaching our current vendor roster and a draft delegation acknowledgement for Upstream. Marissa (privacy) will add the DPIA notes and Karthik will confirm processing locations.

Next steps:
- Marissa: please add DPIA summary and mark any items that require HealthPlus countersignature.
- Karthik: confirm EU/US hosting regions for Upstream and whether encryption-at-rest keys are customer-managed.

Jordan
Jordan Blake
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Amrita Shah <amrita.shah@healthplus.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-19 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor roster + onward transfer permissions for Upstream integration

Hi all — attaching what should cover HealthPlus’ ask. Quick notes inline:

- Attachment: vendor_roster_20270418.csv (columns: vendor_name, purpose, country_of_processing, onward_transfer_allowed, contract_link)
- Attachment: redwood-sccs-delegation-ack-20270419.pdf (signed by Redwood Legal; covers onward transfers to Upstream for telemetry/logging only)
- Attachment: dpia-summary-upstream-connector.docx (short DPIA noting data categories, retention, lawful basis, mitigations)

Highlights from the DPIA:
- Data categories: system logs + request metadata; no persistent customer PII is forwarded to Upstream in normal operation.
- Retention: 30 days, automatic purging; retention can be shortened by contract.
- Security controls: TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest for log snapshots, Access control via SCIM roles.

Open items for HealthPlus to confirm:
1) Do you require Upstream to sign your vendor questionnaire or is the SCCs/delegation sufficient?
2) Any specific data residency constraints beyond EU/US handling?

Priya: flagging for any SOC 2 evidence HealthPlus typically asks for (controls mapping).

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Principal Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Legal Ops (HealthPlus) <legal@healthplus.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Amrita Shah <amrita.shah@healthplus.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-20 14:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor roster + onward transfer permissions for Upstream integration

Jordan/Marissa —

Thanks for the quick package. A couple of follow-ups from our side after review:
- The SCCs you provided look acceptable; we need confirmation whether HealthPlus or Upstream should be the contracting counterparty on the connector scope. If Upstream will act as subprocessor to Redwood and only hold logs, HealthPlus prefers Redwood to contractually bind Upstream (delegation + right-to-audit).
- We’d like a one-page attestation from Upstream confirming they will not use logs for model training and will limit access to designated personnel.

If Redwood can provide the attestation and confirm Redwood retains contractual responsibility for Upstream’s processing, Legal can approve and we’ll proceed to procurement. Otherwise, we’ll need Upstream on the legal side.

Please advise on timing — we’d like to close this week to make go-live on 05/10.

Regards,
Claire D. 
Legal Counsel, HealthPlus


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Legal Ops (HealthPlus) <legal@healthplus.com>, Amrita Shah <amrita.shah@healthplus.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Partner Integrations <integrations@upstream.ai>
Date: 2027-04-21 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor roster + onward transfer permissions for Upstream integration

Hi Claire/Amrita —

Confirming Redwood will remain the contracting processor for Upstream’s telemetry/logging access and we will obtain a one-page attestation from Upstream that: they will not use logs for training, will only retain logs for 30 days, and will restrict access to named admin accounts. I’ve looped in Upstream on this thread (Integrations) to provide the attestation; we will deliver a countersigned SCC addendum if required.

Timing: Upstream has indicated they can provide the attestation by EOD 04/22. Once we have that, Marissa/legal will route the SCC addendum for countersignature (DocuSign link included).

Action items summary:
- Upstream: submit attestation (due 2027-04-22).
- Redwood Legal (Marissa): circulate SCC addendum for signature upon receiving attestation.
- HealthPlus Legal: advise if attestation + SCC addendum satisfies your procurement checklist or if Upstream signature is also required on the master services exhibit.

Thanks —
Karthik

Head of Platform Compliance, Redwood Inference
